tldr: Plivo (hosted SMS/voice/WhatsApp API) and Sonner (open-source React toast UI library) are not substitutes — a 'migration' only applies if Plivo was being used to drive in-app feedback rather than actual telecom messaging.
why_switch: Sonner is free and open source — no per-message cost
Zero vendor lock-in for in-app notification UI
Tiny API and excellent DX for React apps
No telecom compliance overhead for purely in-app feedback
Removes a paid dependency for UI-only use cases
what_to_export: Inventory of Plivo call sites that drive in-app UI feedback (if any)
Branding and styling tokens for toasts
List of Plivo integrations that should stay (real SMS/voice flows)
export_method: Sonner stores nothing — there is no data export. Audit the codebase for places where Plivo was being invoked purely for UI-style feedback and capture the copy and trigger events. Consult Plivo's and Sonner's official docs for current capabilities.
import_method: Install Sonner in your React app per its official docs, mount the Toaster component, and replace the relevant client-side notification calls with Sonner's toast API. Leave real SMS/voice flows on Plivo. Consult Sonner's official docs for current install and usage steps.
effort: 1-3 hours (only if Plivo was used for UI feedback)
gotchas: Different categories — do not remove Plivo if you actually need SMS, voice, or WhatsApp
Sonner is browser-only; it cannot reach a phone number
Toasts auto-dismiss — don't use them for critical confirmations
Mind accessibility (ARIA live regions, reduced motion)
Keep server-side audit logs that Plivo previously provided

email.subject: Switching in-app Plivo feedback to Sonner toasts
email.body:
Hi team,

Where Plivo is being used purely for in-app user feedback, we should move those spots to Sonner — a free open-source React toast library. Real SMS/voice/WhatsApp flows stay on Plivo.

Scope: audit UI-only call sites, install Sonner, mount the Toaster, swap calls for toast.success / toast.error. Estimated 1-3 hours.

Thanks
